content.problemStatement:
Chef's website has a specific response mechanism based on the HTTP status code received:


If the response code is 404404404, the website will return `NOT FOUND`.
For any other response code different from 404404404, the website will return `FOUND`.
Given the response code as XXX, determine the website response.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a response code XXX.


Output Format
Output on a new line `NOT FOUND`, if the response code is 404404404. Otherwise print `FOUND`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `FOUND`, `fouND`, `FouND`, and `found` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer XXX representing the response code.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line "NOT FOUND" if XXX equals 404404404, otherwise output "FOUND".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First code 200 is not the special code, so output FOUND. Second code equals 404404404, so output NOT FOUND. Third code 301 is different, so output FOUND.
404 is not 404404404, therefore FOUND. The second code matches the special value, therefore NOT FOUND.
0 does not equal 404404404, so the website returns FOUND.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the response code as a string (or integer). Compare it with the constant "404404404". If they are equal print "NOT FOUND", else print "FOUND". The comparison is O(1) per test case, so the total complexity is linear in T.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
